title: Trump sinks lower into nepotism with fund to compensate allies
subhead: Acting attorney general Todd Blanche announced the creation of a fund totaling $1.776 billion for Trump's prosecuted allies and established a 'permanent' ban on tax audits into the president and his family.

title: In a tsunami of data leaks, French society remains vulnerable and authorities powerless
EU agrees to implement long-delayed trade pact with Washington
'Heat strokes are part of the job': In Delhi, construction workers endure 43°C heat
subhead: India's capital has faced scorching days since April. On construction sites, workers have tried to adapt but many have fallen victim to heatstroke.

title: Spanish PM Sanchez more weakened than ever as investigation targets predecessor Zapatero
subhead: The summoning of former prime minister José Luis Rodriguez Zapatero by the Spanish judiciary in an influence-peddling case comes at the worst possible time for Pedro Sanchez, who was already weakened by a string of electoral defeats and corruption scandals.

title: Robin Campillo, filmmaker: 'I don't see how a blacklist of French cinema could be enforced. Would it even be legal?'
subhead: The director is among the latest figures to sign the open letter criticizing conservative billionaire Vincent Bolloré, now backed by more than 2,000 industry professionals. He emphasizes the importance of unity, one year ahead of the 2027 French presidential election.

title: 'A more arbitrary and extreme regime': In the West Bank, Israel toughens death penalty law for Palestinians
subhead: A coalition of Israeli and Palestinian non-profit organizations has called for the cancellation of the 'military order' that brought the measure into force, expanding the crimes punishable by death and reducing the means of defense.

The EU's unflinching collaboration with Libya and Tunisia to contain the flow of migrants
subhead: 'Migrants in the Mediterranean: The mechanics of silence' (2/4). Giorgia Meloni's government, backed by the European Union, is funding, equipping and training both the Libyan and Tunisian coast guards – even though these forces have committed rights abuses – to intercept people in exile on their way to Europe.

title: How Le Pen's party has shifted its economic platform over 15 years
subhead: Ever since Marine Le Pen took control of the far-right Rassemblement National in 2011, it has struggled to present a clear economic platform, to the point of muddying its political identity and sparking questions about its plans.

title: As Israeli elections approach, the opposition focuses on rejecting Netanyahu rather than defining peace
subhead: Even as the current prime minister's coalition appears to be in deep trouble, most of his rivals have not dared to break with his nationalist rhetoric, and seem trapped in a conceptual dead end when it comes to coexistence with Palestinians – whether Israeli citizens, residents of Gaza, or the West Bank.
